description:
You are a QA engineer who has to provision a very small, local database-driven test environment so that an automated CI pipeline can later run regression tests against it.  
    Your goal is to create a self-contained SQLite workspace under the directory /home/user/qa_tests/db and then leave behind a reproducible artefact and a verification log file.  
    Everything must be doable from a normal, unprivileged shell session and should take no more than 5-10 shell commands in total (not counting obvious cd or ls checks).  

    STEP-BY-STEP REQUIREMENTS
    ------------------------------------------------------------
    1. Workspace  
       • Create the directory /home/user/qa_tests/db if it does not exist.  
       • All files you create for this task must live inside that directory.  

    2. SQLite database  
       • Name of the database file: test_env.db (place it directly inside the directory above).  
       • Using SQLite, create a single table named users with the exact schema shown below (order and types matter):  
            id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT  
            name        TEXT    NOT NULL  
            email       TEXT    UNIQUE NOT NULL  
            created_at  TEXT    NOT NULL  
       • Insert exactly three rows whose data you freely choose, but make sure:  
            – The primary keys are 1, 2 and 3 (you can omit them in INSERT so that AUTOINCREMENT assigns them).  
            – Emails must be unique.  
            – The created_at column values are ISO-8601 timestamps (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS).  

    3. SQL dump  
       • Export (dump) the full schema and data of test_env.db into a file called test_env_seed.sql in the same directory.  
       • The dump file must be plain ASCII text that can be fed back into sqlite3 to recreate the database.  

    4. Verification query & log  
       • Run a single SQL query that returns the total number of rows in the users table.  
       • Capture its output to a log file called verification.log placed in /home/user/qa_tests/db.  
       • The log file MUST match the exact two-line format below so that the automated grader can parse it:  

            === Row Count Check Start ===
            ROW_COUNT:<number>

         where <number> is the integer count returned by your query (it must obviously be 3 once you have inserted the correct data).  
       • No extra blank lines or spaces are allowed in this log file.  

    5. Idempotency note  
       • Your commands should be written (or chosen) so that if they are run a second time, they do not crash or leave duplicate data. For example, use “IF NOT EXISTS” when creating the table.  

    VERIFICATION HINTS THE GRADER WILL FOLLOW
    ------------------------------------------------------------
    • It will check that /home/user/qa_tests/db/test_env.db exists and is a valid SQLite file.  
    • It will connect to that database, ensure the users table exists with the exact schema (column names, order, and types must match).  
    • It will verify that exactly three rows are stored and that their id values are 1, 2, 3.  
    • It will read /home/user/qa_tests/db/verification.log and confirm it exactly contains the two lines specified above with the number 3.  
    • It will feed /home/user/qa_tests/db/test_env_seed.sql into an empty SQLite database and expect to obtain a clone that also contains the users table with three rows.

    PLEASE DO NOT DELETE ANY FILES YOU CREATE; THEY ARE NEEDED BY THE GRADER.
truth:
Directory that must exist (created by agent):
        /home/user/qa_tests/db

    Files that must exist at grading time:
        1) /home/user/qa_tests/db/test_env.db
           • A valid SQLite 3 database.
           • Must contain exactly one table 'users' with four columns:
                 id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
                 name TEXT NOT NULL
                 email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL
                 created_at TEXT NOT NULL
           • Exactly three rows, ids 1,2,3.  Example values (grader will only check constraints):
                 1 | Alice  | alice@example.com  | 2023-01-01T10:00:00
                 2 | Bob    | bob@example.com    | 2023-01-02T11:00:00
                 3 | Charlie| charlie@demo.net   | 2023-01-03T12:00:00

        2) /home/user/qa_tests/db/test_env_seed.sql
           • Plain text dump produced by "sqlite3 test_env.db .dump".
           • Must include the CREATE TABLE statement for users and three INSERT statements.

        3) /home/user/qa_tests/db/verification.log
           • Exactly two lines, ASCII, newline-terminated:
                 === Row Count Check Start ===
                 ROW_COUNT:3

    The grader will also:
        • Run "sqlite3 /home/user/qa_tests/db/test_env.db 'PRAGMA integrity_check;'" expecting "ok".
        • Run "sqlite3 < /home/user/qa_tests/db/test_env_seed.sql" on a temp db and expect same schema + 3 rows.
